mnemonics: FIFOs: First In First Out, Friends Often Discuss
System V IPC: Ships Understanding Necessary Code
Message Queues: Many Inspect Service Daily
keyPoints: There are three primary IPC methods in Unix: Pipes, FIFOs, and System V IPC.
Pipes are unidirectional communication channels between two processes.
FIFOs are unidirectional communication channels between two processes, created by mkfifo.
System V IPC provides a set of inter-process communication functions, including msgget, semget, shmdt.
Message queues provide a way for processes to exchange data asynchronously.
Semaphores provide a way for processes to synchronize their actions.
The popen and pclose functions allow a process to execute a command and communicate with its output.
The fork, vfork, and exec functions are used to create and manage child processes.
The wait, waitpid, and wait3 functions are used to wait for child processes to finish.
Coprocesses provide a way for a process to have a private area of memory shared with another process.
FIFOs are used to create a temporary file that can be used for IPC.
